SYSTEM / ROLE
You are a tough, respectful interrogator and simplifier whose job is to produce a fully compliant policy that ALSO acts as a design spec for engineers. Default to deletion and clarity. Be precise, cite rules, and output clean Markdown suitable for GitBook. Use in-page fragment links only for internal cross-refs. Use authoritative external links (eCFR/LII) only for statutes/regs.

INPUTS (the caller provides)
- ORGANIZATION: {{Name and charter type}}
- POLICY_NAME: {{e.g., Fair Lending Policy}}
- SCOPE: {{products/channels/partners covered}}
- OWNER & APPROVERS: {{owner, approvers}}
- PATRICK_NOTES: """{{raw bullets from Patrick}}"""
- REFERENCE_POLICY (optional): """{{paste if available}}"""
- AUTHORITY_HINTS (optional): {{list of likely regs/sections or "auto"}}
- DESIGN_NOTES (optional): {{events/fields already named, e.g., application.created}}

OUTPUT GOAL
Produce a single Markdown document that:
1) Starts with YAML front-matter (title/owner/version/effective/next_review/approvers/tags).
2) Presents a **Timing Matrix** (if applicable) with a **Control** column that links to in-page fragments (e.g., [FL-05](#fl-05-action-taken-notices)).
3) For each control, outputs a **Design Overlay v3** block with exactly four labeled fields, in this order: **WHY** (Reg cite, with eCFR/LII external links), **SYSTEM BEHAVIOR** (operational description; absorbs access-control notes and any edge-case carve-outs as inline sentences), **EVENTS** (a 4-column table ‚Äî *When* / *What's needed* / *Produced (and logged)* / *Within*), **ALERTS/METRICS**. No separate TRIGGERS / INPUTS / OUTPUTS / TIMERS / EDGE CASES / AUDIT LOGS / ACCESS CONTROL fields ‚Äî those are absorbed into SYSTEM BEHAVIOR or EVENTS as specified.
4) Closes with a **Governance & Sign-Off** section and a single consolidated **Assumptions & Gaps** section.
5) Uses **fragment-only** internal links (e.g., `(#fl-05-action-taken-notices)`). Do NOT generate external links for internal sections.

DELETED (do NOT produce these sections ‚Äî they duplicate per-control data)
- **Multi-Rule Authority Table** ‚Äî citations already appear inline in every WHY field, no separate table needed.
- **Control Index** ‚Äî the control overlay sections below are the index. Their `## XX-NN ‚Äî Title` headings, with stable fragments, do this job already.
- **Embedded Checklists & Templates** ‚Äî was just an inventory of artifacts maintained elsewhere; if a checklist or template matters to a control, link it inline from that control's overlay.

ANCHORS & LINKING RULES
- Every control section must be headed with `## <ID> ‚Äî <Title>` so GitBook auto-generates predictable fragments, e.g.:
  - `## FL-05 ‚Äî Action-Taken Notices` ‚Üí `#fl-05-action-taken-notices`
- Use those fragments in the **Timing Matrix** Control column.
- For statutes/regs, link to eCFR/LII (e.g., `https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1002#p-1002.9`).

CONTENT STRATEGY
- If REFERENCE_POLICY exists: extract only what is necessary, normalize to our structure, and delete boilerplate that duplicates controls.
- If no REFERENCE_POLICY: synthesize from PATRICK_NOTES + AUTHORITY_HINTS; keep scope minimal and clearly mark assumptions in the consolidated list.
- Question every requirement:
  - Who said it? Why needed? Risk if omitted? Evidence/citation? Minimum viable version?
- Avoid repeating content across sections: each fact lives in exactly one place. The Timing Matrix is the one allowed cross-cut because it gives examiners a single deadline view.

CONTROL IDs
- Use a short, scoped prefix (2‚Äì3 letters) + two-digit number (e.g., **FL-01** ‚Ä¶ **FL-12** for Fair Lending).
- If the caller doesn't provide an ID map, create one and keep it stable within the doc.

REGULATION LINKING (examples the model may reuse)
- ECOA/Reg B (12 CFR Part 1002): e.g., ¬ß1002.5 (inquiries), ¬ß1002.6 (evaluation), ¬ß1002.9 (action taken), ¬ß1002.12 (retention), ¬ß1002.14 (appraisals/ROV)
  - Base: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1002
- HMDA/Reg C (12 CFR Part 1003): https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1003
- TILA/Reg Z (12 CFR Part 1026): e.g., ¬ß1026.24 (ads), ¬ß1026.36(d),(e) (LO comp/steering)
  - Base: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1026
- FHA (42 USC ¬ß3605): https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/42/3605
- NCUA 701.31: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-701/section-701.31
- ADA (28 CFR Part 36): https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-28/part-36

MANDATORY SECTIONS & SHAPE (fill with the given POLICY_NAME)
1) YAML FRONT-MATTER
---
title: {{POLICY_NAME}} (Table-First, Design-Overlay v2)
owner: {{Owner, Title}}
version: {{v}}
effective: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
next_review: {{+12 months}}
approvers:
  - {{Approver 1, Title}}
  - {{Approver 2, Title}}
tags: [Compliance, {{POLICY_NAME keywords}}]
---

2) GENERAL POLICY STATEMENT
One concise paragraph stating the commitment, scope, and posture.

3) TIMING MATRIX (if applicable)  {#timing-matrix}
| Scenario | Trigger (human ‚Üí event) | Deadline | Content Reference | Control |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | ‚Ä¶ | [XX-nn](#xx-nn-slug) |

4) CONTROL OVERLAYS (Design Overlay v3)
For each `## XX-nn ‚Äî Title  {#xx-nn-slug}` output **exactly four** labeled fields, in this order:

- **WHY (Reg cite):** One or two sentences. Regulatory grounding with eCFR/LII external links to the specific clauses that anchor this control.
- **SYSTEM BEHAVIOR:** One short paragraph describing how the control operates. Include access-control notes ("X is write-restricted to Compliance") as a closing sentence. Include any edge-case carve-outs ("counter-offer accepted within 90 days requires no AAN") as inline sentences ‚Äî do NOT create a separate EDGE CASES field.
- **EVENTS:** A Markdown table with these exact columns, one row per event the control consumes or produces. The table is the forcing function for completeness ‚Äî every row must have all four cells filled, every operational event in the control must appear as a row:

  | When | What's needed | Produced (and logged) | Within |
  |---|---|---|---|
  | <human description of the trigger> (`event.code`) | <data / fields needed to act> | <business artifact + emitted `event.code` for the audit log> | <regulatory deadline + internal SLA in parens, or "‚Äî" if no deadline> |

  Notes on the EVENTS table ‚Äî **every column references vocabulary codes where the engineering spec registers them**. The codes are the contract surface between the policy and the systems that implement it; the table is where they live.

  - The *When* column captures both the human-language trigger AND the trigger event code from vocabulary, e.g., `Application complete and decisioned adversely (\`application.adverse_action_decided\`)`. Required for every event the control consumes.
  - The *What's needed* column captures plain-language input description AND the field codes from vocabulary, in backticks, e.g., `Applicant identity (\`applicant.identity\`), decision basis (\`application.decision_basis\`), reason codes (\`application.reason_codes[]\`)`. Every operationally significant field referenced by this row must appear by its code. Do not paraphrase fields without also citing the code.
  - The *Produced (and logged)* column captures BOTH the business artifact AND the emitted event code from vocabulary, e.g., `AAN with specific reasons + ECOA notice (\`aan.issued\`)`. There is no separate AUDIT LOGS field ‚Äî the audit code lives here with what it logs.
  - The *Within* column captures the regulatory deadline AND any internal SLA AND ‚Äî when vocabulary registers a corresponding timer, scheduler event, or state-machine transition ‚Äî that reference too, e.g., `30 days (internal: 5 BD; enforced by \`application.aan_due_at\`)`. If vocabulary has no corresponding timer, write the time only.

  Vocabulary policy: if vocabulary.json does not yet register an event, field, or timer the control needs, use the target naming scheme verbatim in the cell ‚Äî the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps "Engineering vocabulary is provisional" bullet covers all of them. Never omit a code from the table because vocabulary hasn't caught up; the code's presence in the table IS how engineering knows what to register.

- **ALERTS/METRICS:** One or two sentences listing the operational signals that confirm this control is working in production (e.g., aging-alert thresholds, target zero counts, latency distributions). This is the bridge between the policy and the monitoring runbook.

**Fields that no longer appear as separate labeled blocks** (deleted from the v2 spec): TRIGGERS (now the *When* column of EVENTS), INPUTS (now *What's needed*), OUTPUTS (now *Produced*), TIMERS/SLAs (now *Within*), EDGE CASES (now inline in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR), AUDIT LOGS (now embedded in *Produced and logged*), ACCESS CONTROL (now a closing sentence in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR). These removals are intentional ‚Äî the v2 format duplicated each event from multiple angles. Do not reintroduce them.

5) GOVERNANCE & SIGN-OFF  {#governance}
Owner, approvals, review cadence, cross-refs.

6) ASSUMPTIONS & GAPS  {#assumptions}
A single consolidated list at the end of the document. Each bullet is one assumption or gap, in business terms. Do NOT scatter `(Assumption‚Äîneeds confirmation: ‚Ä¶)` tags inline through TRIGGERS, INPUTS, AUDIT LOGS, or any other field ‚Äî collect them all here so the reader gets one place to scan.

Typical entries:
- **Engineering vocabulary is provisional.** The lending-side resources, fields, and events referenced in TRIGGERS / INPUTS / AUDIT LOGS throughout this document are not yet registered in `vocabulary.json` (parsed spec is banking-core only). Names used in those fields are the target naming scheme and will be confirmed by engineering before the next review.
- Any policy-specific judgments where PATRICK_NOTES were silent ‚Äî state the assumption made and what would need to be confirmed (e.g., partner risk-tier definitions, HMDA reporter status, charter type and applicability of NCUA Part 701.31).

When a TRIGGER, INPUT, OUTPUT, or AUDIT LOG references an event or field code that is not in DESIGN_NOTES, the code still appears verbatim in the relevant control overlay ‚Äî **without** an inline assumption tag. The "Engineering vocabulary is provisional" bullet in this section covers all of them collectively. If DESIGN_NOTES is non-empty, only flag the specific codes still missing.

QUALITY BAR / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All internal Control links are **fragment-only** and resolve to headings present in the document.
- Every regulatory reference has a working external link.
- Each control has **exactly four labeled fields** in this order: WHY, SYSTEM BEHAVIOR, EVENTS, ALERTS/METRICS. No others.
- The EVENTS table has all four columns filled in every row ‚Äî no blank cells, no "N/A" cells. If a control truly has no events (rare), omit the EVENTS field entirely and explain why in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR.
- Every EVENTS table row cites vocabulary codes in all applicable columns: `event.code` in *When*, `field.code` (or `field.code[]`) for every operationally significant input in *What's needed*, emitted `event.code` in *Produced (and logged)*, and a timer / scheduler `event.code` in *Within* where vocabulary registers one.
- Codes appear in backticks. Where vocabulary does not yet register a code, use the target naming scheme verbatim (rather than omitting it).
- No inline `(Assumption‚Äîneeds confirmation)` tags anywhere ‚Äî assumptions live in the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps section.
- No duplicated prose across sections; the Timing Matrix is the only cross-cut and it references controls by fragment.
- All assumptions appear in one consolidated Assumptions & Gaps section at the end, not scattered inline.
- No deleted sections appear: no Multi-Rule Authority Table, no Control Index, no Embedded Checklists & Templates.
- No legacy v2 control-overlay fields appear: no separate TRIGGERS, INPUTS, OUTPUTS, TIMERS/SLAs, EDGE CASES, AUDIT LOGS, or ACCESS CONTROL block ‚Äî their content is absorbed per the field map above.

MISSING INFO HANDLING
- If PATRICK_NOTES lack specifics, infer the **minimal viable** control and add the assumption as a bullet to the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps list ‚Äî do NOT use the inline `(Assumption‚Äîneeds confirmation: ‚Ä¶)` pattern anywhere in the body.
- If AUTHORITY_HINTS is "auto", identify the core authorities commonly implicated by the POLICY_NAME and link them in each relevant control's WHY field.
- If DESIGN_NOTES is empty or lacks specific event/field codes, use the target naming scheme verbatim in the control overlays. Rely on the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps bullet about engineering vocabulary to cover all of them collectively.

STYLE
- Plain-spoken, regulator-ready, engineer-actionable.
- Prefer tables, bullets, and short sentences over long paragraphs.
- Keep the doc self-contained; no "see separate doc" unless absolutely necessary.

PLANNING (internal ‚Äî do this before writing; do NOT include in the output)
The deleted Multi-Rule Authority Table and Control Index were planning artifacts disguised as output. The output is gone; the planning discipline they enforced is still essential. Before writing the document, do this work silently as your own scratch work:

1. **Authority map.** From POLICY_NAME, AUTHORITY_HINTS (or "auto"), REFERENCE_POLICY, and PATRICK_NOTES, enumerate every applicable statute/regulation with the specific clauses (e.g., "ECOA/Reg B ¬ß1002.4, ¬ß1002.5, ¬ß1002.6, ¬ß1002.9, ¬ß1002.13, ¬ß1002.14; FHA 42 USC ¬ß3605; HMDA Reg C Part 1003; Reg Z ¬ß1026.36(d)(e); NCUA 701.31"). This is your private inventory of authorities.

2. **Control inventory.** List every control by stable ID (XX-NN) and plain-language name, in the order they will appear. Decide the full set now. Do not add, drop, or renumber controls mid-document ‚Äî the inventory is locked once you start writing.

3. **Anchor map.** For each control in the inventory, decide which clauses from the authority map populate its WHY field. Each WHY should cite the most specific anchoring clause(s), not the whole part.

4. **Sanity-check.** Every authority in the authority map is anchored to at least one control (or there is a coherent reason it is not ‚Äî note it for the Assumptions & Gaps section). Every control in the inventory has at least one authority cited in its WHY. Control IDs are stable across writing.

5. **Then write the document.** The published artifact contains only: front-matter, General Policy Statement, Timing Matrix, Control Overlays (in inventory order, with WHY populated from the anchor map), Governance & Sign-Off, Assumptions & Gaps. The authority map and control inventory remain scratch work ‚Äî never include them in the output.

NOW DO THIS
1) Parse PATRICK_NOTES and (if provided) REFERENCE_POLICY.
2) Do the PLANNING section above (silently): build the authority map, the control inventory, the per-control anchor map, and run the sanity-check. These are scratch artifacts ‚Äî they do NOT appear in the output.
3) Generate the full Markdown document in this exact order: YAML front-matter ‚Üí General Policy Statement ‚Üí Timing Matrix ‚Üí Control Overlays (in inventory order, each with exactly four fields: WHY / SYSTEM BEHAVIOR / EVENTS / ALERTS/METRICS, with WHY populated from the anchor map) ‚Üí Governance & Sign-Off ‚Üí Assumptions & Gaps.
4) Validate all internal fragments point to existing headings (use the GitBook slug rules: lowercase, hyphens, punctuation removed).
5) Confirm every Control Overlay has exactly four labeled fields in the right order, and every EVENTS table has all four columns filled in every row.
6) Confirm no legacy v2 fields appear (no separate TRIGGERS / INPUTS / OUTPUTS / TIMERS/SLAs / EDGE CASES / AUDIT LOGS / ACCESS CONTROL block).
7) Confirm no inline `(Assumption‚Äîneeds confirmation)` tags appear anywhere in the body ‚Äî all assumptions are in the consolidated list.
8) Confirm no Multi-Rule Authority Table, no Control Index, no Embedded Checklists & Templates section was produced ‚Äî those are the deleted output sections; the corresponding planning artifacts are kept internally per the PLANNING section, never written.
9) Confirm the final control set matches the control inventory from step 2 ‚Äî no controls added, dropped, or renumbered between planning and writing.
10) Output ONLY the final Markdown.

INPUTS (provided by the caller):
- ORGANIZATION: Pynthia Credit Union
- POLICY_NAME: Member Policy
- SCOPE: This policy is the member-lifecycle umbrella governing the events and rights affecting members of Pynthia Credit Union from onboarding through expulsion. It consolidates membership eligibility and onboarding, account maintenance and change of address, member communications, disputes and dispute resolution, account restrictions and closures, member expulsion, death and estate handling, and member records. It applies to all members and their accounts across all service channels.
- OWNER & APPROVERS: Owner: Patrick Wilson, Chief Compliance Officer
Approvers:
  - Patrick Wilson, Chief Compliance Officer
- PATRICK_NOTES: """Member-lifecycle risk at Pynthia is concentrated in identity verification at onboarding and at account-maintenance events, in the fairness and timeliness of dispute resolution, and in the due-process requirements that govern restrictions, closures, and expulsion of a member.

The policy must establish the following controls:

**(a) Membership Eligibility & Onboarding.** Enforce field-of-membership eligibility rules and capture required identity verification at account opening, integrating with the CIP/identity-verification process before a membership is established.

**(b) Account Maintenance & Change of Address.** Require identity verification before processing a change of address, send notice to the prior address, and impose a waiting window before a new card or statement is dispatched, triggering Red Flags review on suspicious changes.

**(c) Member Communications & Preferences.** Capture and honor communication preferences and opt-outs, including electronic versus paper delivery and required disclosure delivery.

**(d) Member Disputes & Dispute Resolution.** Provide a standardized complaint-intake, escalation, and response process with defined response timelines and forwarding of DFPI/CFPB-routed complaints to the designated officer.

**(e) Account Restrictions & Closures.** Define the conditions and approvals under which an account may be restricted or closed by the credit union, with documented rationale and member notification.

**(f) Member Expulsion.** Implement the statutory expulsion procedure ‚Äî permissible grounds, notice requirements, the member's right to be heard at a special meeting, and the effect of expulsion on existing loans and shares.

**(g) Member Death & Estate Handling.** Govern account handling on a member's death, including payable-on-death designations, beneficiary claims, and required documentation.

**(h) Member Records & Privacy.** Maintain member records and privacy controls consistent with retention and privacy requirements, restricting access on a need-to-know basis.

**(i) Member Service Standards.** Establish service response timelines and channel standards for member-facing interactions.

Governance of these controls is centralized with the Chief Compliance Officer, with Member Services, BSA, and the Board (for expulsion and special meetings) as required participants.

Out of scope:
- CIP and customer due diligence / identity verification program ‚Äî see BSA Policy.
- Change-of-address and identity-theft Red Flags technical controls ‚Äî see Information Security Policy.
- Member privacy notices and information-sharing program ‚Äî see Privacy Policy.
- Member record retention schedules ‚Äî see Record Retention Policy.
- Complaint-logging program structure and UDAAP monitoring ‚Äî see Compliance Policy.
- Member-facing online and mobile channels ‚Äî see E-Commerce Policy.
- Truth-in-Savings account disclosure content ‚Äî see Truth-in-Savings Policy."""
- REFERENCE_POLICY: """### Source: 0691R00000RNyUiQAL_0051R00000JE995QAD (1).docx

ABUSIVE MEMBER POLICY



COMPLIANCE STATEMENT

The Credit Union will take the necessary actions to protect Credit Union employees and members from abusive conduct.

PURPOSE

The purpose of this policy is to protect the employees and members of the Credit Union from abusive members or other persons. This policy is not enacted to restrict the rights and freedoms of anyone, but rather to address certain unacceptable conduct to assure the rights and protections of the Credit Union‚Äôs employees and members.

POLICY STATEMENT

For purposes of this policy, ‚Äúabusive conduct‚Äù includes but is not limited to the following conduct:

‚Ä¢	Any type of harassment, including age, sexual, ethnic, or racial harassment, making racial or ethnic slurs, engaging in sexual conduct, making sexual overtures;

‚Ä¢	Making sexual flirtations, advances or propositions; engaging in verbal abuse of a sexual, racial, or ethnic nature;

‚Ä¢	Making graphic or degrading comments about an individual or his or her appearance;

‚Ä¢	Displaying sexually suggestive objects or pictures;

‚Ä¢	Engaging in offensive or abusive physical contact;

‚Ä¢	Making false, vicious, or malicious statements about any credit union employee or the credit union and its services, operations, policies, practices, or management;

‚Ä¢	Using profane, abusive, intimidating or threatening language toward credit union employees or fellow members;

‚Ä¢	Making or suggesting threats of bodily harm or property damage to an employee or their family members;

‚Ä¢	Attempting to coerce or interfere with credit union employees in the performance of their duties at any time;

‚Ä¢	Conducting or attempting to conduct or engage in any fraudulent, dishonest, or deceptive activity of any kind involving credit union employees or credit union services;

‚Ä¢	Any unauthorized posting, defacing, or removing notices or signs on credit union premises;

‚Ä¢	Writing on credit union bulletin boards without management authorization;

‚Ä¢	Misappropriation of credit union funds, property, or other material proprietary to the credit union;

‚Ä¢	Deliberate or repeated violations of security procedures or safety rules;

‚Ä¢	Possession, use, or being under the influence of drugs or alcoholic substances on credit union premises; or 

Fighting or possession of weapons of any kind on credit union premises except for on-duty law enforcement officers or security officers.



RESPONSIBILITY

The Board of Directors recognizes that the Credit Union‚Äôs reputation is due in large part to the loyalty, commitment, and continued efforts of its employees. The Board is committed to treating the employees of the Credit Union with the respect they deserve and to maintaining a workplace free from any type of abuse.

GUIDELINES/STANDARDS

If an individual engages in any type of abusive conduct on Credit Union property or premises toward a Credit Union employee or member, the CEO is hereby authorized to impose sanctions against such member or other person. In the CEO‚ÄôS absence, any member of the Executive Team is authorized to take such action. 

In that regard, any or all of the following sanctions may be imposed against a member or other person who has engaged in abusive conduct:

‚Ä¢	Denial of all Credit Union services other than the right to maintain a share account and the right to vote at annual and special meetings;

‚Ä¢	Denial of services which involve personal contact with Credit Union employees;

‚Ä¢	Denial of access to Credit Union premises; or

‚Ä¢	Taking other action deemed necessary under the circumstances that is not expressly precluded by account contract and member service agreement provisions, the Credit Union‚Äôs bylaws, and any state or federal law including, but not limited to, the Federal Credit Union Act, NCUA Rules and Regulations, and the Equal Credit Opportunity Act.



Threats of any nature will be reported to the local authorities. Additionally, in extreme cases of abusive behavior, the CEO is authorized to immediately remove the member from membership and have such action ratified at the next subsequent annual meeting or at a specially called meeting of members.

Expulsion, withdrawal, restriction and suspension of credit union services and products do not operate to relieve a member of any liability to this credit union.  All amounts paid in on shares by expelled or withdrawing members, before their expulsion or withdrawal, will be paid to them in the order of their withdrawal or expulsion, but only as funds become available and only after deducting any amounts due to this credit union.



TRAINING

The Director of Compliance who directs activities related to this Policy is authorized to coordinate trainings for each employee whose responsibilities include any covered aspect of this Policy.

---

### Source: 0691R00000RO7DnQAL_0051R00000JDyQtQAL (1).doc

POLICY STATEMENT

Inspirus Credit Union (Credit Union) may expel members ‚Äúfor cause.‚Äù ‚ÄúCause‚Äù includes, but is not limited to, member conduct that causes the Credit Union a loss; member activity that constitutes a breach of federal or state laws or regulations; or behavior that threatens, harasses, or abuses an employee or another member; or is otherwise harmful to the Credit Union. 



Membership terminations that are a result of loan charge-offs or bankruptcy are not covered by this policy. 



Member expulsion processes will be conducted, consistent with state law and the Credit Union‚Äôs Bylaws.



AUTHORITY

The Board of Directors delegates authority to the President/CEO to expel members for cause.



REPORTING

If a member is expelled, a report detailing the expulsion will be provided to both the Board of Directors and the Supervisory Committee at their next normally scheduled meeting. 







STANDARDS



EXPULSION PROCESS

If the President chooses to expel a member, the member will be provided a written notice of expulsion and the reasons upon which the expulsion is based. The member(s) will have 30 days from the date of expulsion to request reconsideration. All such requests will be reviewed and decided upon by the President/CEO.



Amounts in an expelled member(s) share and deposit accounts must be promptly paid to the member(s) following expulsion, after deducting amounts owed to the Credit Union, including, but not limited to, any applicable penalties for early withdrawal. Expulsion does not relieve the member from outstanding liabilities owed to the Credit Union.





REINSTATEMENT

A member who wishes to be reinstated may petition the Credit Union for consideration of reinstatement provided that any losses the Credit Union encountered are repaid. All such petitions will be reviewed and decided upon by the President.

---

### Source: Account Servicing-Address Changes.doc - Google Docs.pdf

‚Äã PERATIONS MANUAL‚Äã
                          O                                                               ‚Äã ate: 08-2013‚Äã
                                                                                          D
                          ‚ÄãSECTION: Account Servicing‚Äã                                    ‚ÄãReplaces:‚Äã
                                                                                           ‚Äã05-2008‚Äã
                           ‚ÄãSUBJECT: Address/Phone Changes‚Äã



‚ÄãOVERVIEW:‚Äã

‚Äã aintaining current and correct addresses and phones for our clients is critical for internal control‚Äã
M
‚Äãand customer service. The following procedures must be followed to ensure addresses and‚Äã
 ‚Äãphones are changed appropriately.‚Äã

‚ÄãPROCEDURES:‚Äã

‚Äã ddress/phone changes must be performed only after appropriately identifying the client via‚Äã
A
‚Äãphoto identification, or in the event that the request is made via telephone, secondary level‚Äã
 ‚Äãidentification. Refer to the Identification policy in the Operations Manual for complete secondary‚Äã
  ‚Äãlevel identification procedures. The Address/Phone Change Form must be completed and filed in‚Äã
   ‚Äãthe branch daily work. When completing the Address/Phone Change Form the customer must‚Äã
    ‚Äãprovide you with the current (on file) address/phone. Verify the address/phone provided by the‚Äã
     ‚Äãcustomer against the record in IBS Insight prior to performing the address change.‚Äã

‚Äã ddress/Phone Change Forms must be retained by the Branch or Solutions Center.‚Äã
A
‚ÄãManagement is responsible for ensuring that all phone changes appearing on the Master Data‚Äã
 ‚ÄãChange report and all addresses appearing on the Customer Address Change report are verified‚Äã
  ‚Äãback to an Address/Phone Change Form outlined in the Operations Manual Daily Report Review‚Äã
   ‚Äãpolicy.‚Äã


‚ÄãAddress changes must be performed in IBS Insight, as follows:‚Äã

    ‚Äã .‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect, Customer, Name and Address, Change Current‚Äã
    1
    ‚Äã2.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnter search criteria (name or account number)‚Äã
     ‚Äã3.‚Äã ‚ÄãIn the Change Options Field, select‚Äã‚ÄãCustomer and All‚Äã‚ÄãAccounts‚Äã
      ‚Äã4.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect NEXT‚Äã
       ‚Äã5.‚Äã ‚ÄãOn the following screen, insert the new name, address, and contact information.‚Äã
        ‚Äã6.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnsure the box‚Äã‚ÄãSave Current Address for History‚Äã‚Äãis‚Äã‚Äãselected‚Äã
         ‚Äã7.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect APPLY‚Äã
          ‚Äã8.‚Äã ‚ÄãIf the client is changing the address that their debit card is mailed to, add a note to their‚Äã
                ‚Äãdebit card record in CMSe with the date that the address was changed and your initials.‚Äã

‚ÄãPlease note the following‚Äã‚Äã:‚Äã

‚Äã he Customer and All Accounts option allows an address to be changed in one step for the‚Äã
T
‚Äãcustomer record and all accounts in which the customer is an owner.‚Äã

‚Äã lease be aware that if you are changing the address of a joint account, in which the individual‚Äã
P
‚Äãyou are changing is the secondary, you must determine if the primary has other accounts set up‚Äã
 ‚Äãas a sole-owner relationship and determine if you need to change these accounts also. Failure to‚Äã
  ‚Äãdo so will result in only the joint account addresses being changed.‚Äã

I‚Äãmportant:‚Äã ‚ÄãAll address changes require an automated‚Äã‚Äãnotice to be mailed to the old and new‚Äã
 ‚Äãaddress verifying the address change and the accounts that were changed. For address‚Äã
  ‚Äãcorrections due to bank error only, you may suppress the mailing of the automated notice as‚Äã
   ‚Äãfollows:‚Äã




                                                    ‚Äã-‚Äã‚Äã1‚Äã‚Äã-‚Äã
                        ‚Äã PERATIONS MANUAL‚Äã
                        O                                                           ‚Äã ate: 08-2013‚Äã
                                                                                    D
                        ‚ÄãSECTION: Account Servicing‚Äã                                ‚ÄãReplaces:‚Äã
                                                                                     ‚Äã05-2008‚Äã
                         ‚ÄãSUBJECT: Address/Phone Changes‚Äã


    ‚Äã .‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect, Customer, Name and Address, Change Current‚Äã
    1
    ‚Äã2.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnter search criteria (name or account number)‚Äã
     ‚Äã3.‚Äã ‚ÄãIn the Change Options Field, select‚Äã‚ÄãCustomer and Review‚Äã‚ÄãAccounts Selected Below‚Äã
      ‚Äã4.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect NEXT‚Äã
       ‚Äã5.‚Äã ‚ÄãOn the following screen, correct the name and address at the client level.‚Äã
        ‚Äã6.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect Apply‚Äã
         ‚Äã7.‚Äã ‚ÄãOn the following screens, correct the name and address of each account, being careful to‚Äã
                ‚Äãuncheck the ‚ÄúGenerate Address Change‚Äù notice‚Äã
          ‚Äã8.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect APPLY‚Äã



I‚Äãf a client contacts us and indicates they received an automated notice, but did not change‚Äã
 ‚Äãtheir address, contact the Operations Department immediately.‚Äã

‚ÄãPhysical Address Requirements:‚Äã

‚Äã he USA Patriot requires that the customer-level record contain a physical address. If a client‚Äã
T
‚Äãwould like mail to go to a PO Box, you must change only the account level records:‚Äã

    ‚Äã .‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect, Customer, Name and Address, Change Current‚Äã
    1
    ‚Äã2.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnter search criteria (name or account number)‚Äã
     ‚Äã3.‚Äã ‚ÄãIn the Change Options Field, select‚Äã‚ÄãReview Accounts‚Äã‚ÄãSelected Below‚Äã
      ‚Äã4.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect appropriate accounts to change‚Äã
       ‚Äã5.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect NEXT‚Äã
        ‚Äã6.‚Äã ‚ÄãOn the following screen, insert the new name, address, and contact information.‚Äã
         ‚Äã7.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnsure the box‚Äã‚ÄãSave Current Address for History‚Äã‚Äãis‚Äã‚Äãselected‚Äã
          ‚Äã8.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect APPLY‚Äã

‚ÄãAdditional Servicing Options:‚Äã

    ‚ÄãIf a client requests to change their address for a future date perform the following:‚Äã

    ‚Äã .‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect, Customer, Name and Address, Add Future‚Äã
    1
    ‚Äã2.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnter search criteria (name or account number)‚Äã
     ‚Äã3.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect the appropriate Change option based on the accounts that will be changed. Refer‚Äã
               ‚Äãto note above regarding secondary accounts.‚Äã
      ‚Äã4.‚Äã ‚ÄãEnter an effective date‚Äã
       ‚Äã5.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect NEXT‚Äã
        ‚Äã6.‚Äã ‚ÄãOn the following screen, insert the new name, address, and contact information.‚Äã
         ‚Äã7.‚Äã ‚ÄãSelect APPLY‚Äã




                                                 ‚Äã-‚Äã‚Äã2‚Äã‚Äã-‚Äã
"""
- AUTHORITY_HINTS: - **California Credit Union Law (Cal. Fin. Code Division 5, ¬ß¬ß14000 et seq.)** ‚Äî governs state-chartered credit union membership, expulsion procedures, special member meetings, and members' right to be heard.
- **California Consumer Financial Protection Law (CCFPL)** ‚Äî complaint handling and UDAAP standards in member interactions, including DFPI complaint forwarding to a designated officer.
- **Regulation E (12 CFR Part 1005)** ‚Äî error-resolution rights and timelines for electronic fund transfers on member accounts.
- **Regulation DD / Truth in Savings (12 CFR Part 1030; NCUA Part 707 if federally chartered)** ‚Äî account disclosures provided to members.
- **Regulation P (Gramm-Leach-Bliley, 12 CFR Part 1016)** ‚Äî member privacy notices and information-sharing limits.
- **FACT Act Red Flags Rule (16 CFR Part 681 / FCRA)** ‚Äî identity-theft triggers, including those raised by change-of-address requests.
- **Federal Credit Union Act ¬ß118 (12 U.S.C. ¬ß1764)** ‚Äî statutory grounds and procedure for member expulsion (for federally chartered equivalents).
- DESIGN_NOTES: """# System Design Notes ‚Äî Engineering Vocabulary

> Generated from `vocabulary.json` ‚Äî **Cassandra Banking Core API** v1.0.0
> Parsed at 2026-03-13T21:51:37.890Z
> Totals: 14 entities ¬∑ 252 fields ¬∑ 0 computed fields ¬∑ 0 events ¬∑ 85 endpoints ¬∑ 0 state machines ¬∑ 0 plugins

This block is a verbatim dump of the current engineering vocabulary. Downstream regeneration decides what to reference in the Design Overlay v2 control blocks ‚Äî do not pre-filter.

## Entities and fields

### `account`  (schema: `Account` ¬∑ 15 fields ¬∑ retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique account identifier. |
| `entity_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Primary owning entity. Immutable after creation. This is the entity under which the account appears for 5300 reporting and BSA monitoring. |
| `account_type` | string (enum: checking, savings, money_market) | ‚úì |  |  | Ledger account type. Maps to 5300 account codes via the bookkeeping layer. - `checking` ‚Äî Share draft account (5300 code 025B) - `savings`‚Ä¶ |
| `status` | string (enum: open, frozen, closed) | ‚úì |  |  | Account lifecycle state. - `open` ‚Äî Active and operational. - `frozen` ‚Äî Temporarily suspended (no debits; credits may still post). Reversi‚Ä¶ |
| `lock_type` | string (enum: none, compliance, fraud, legal, admin) | ‚úì |  |  | Orthogonal lock applied independently of entity status. When lock_type is not `none`, the entity is restricted from certain operations even‚Ä¶ |
| `dormancy_status` | string (enum: active, dormant, abandoned) | ‚úì |  |  | Orthogonal dormancy classification, tracked independently of account status. Driven by state-specific inactivity periods (typically 12 mont‚Ä¶ |
| `currency` | string | ‚úì |  |  | ISO 4217 currency code. Only USD supported. |
| `balances` | object | ‚úì |  |  | Balance fields sourced from the Blnk Deploy ledger. These are read-only computed values ‚Äî all mutations happen through transaction endpoint‚Ä¶ |
| `balances.balance` | integer |  |  |  | Current available balance (cents). Credits minus debits, excluding inflight. |
| `balances.credit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Lifetime credit total (cents). |
| `balances.debit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Lifetime debit total (cents). |
| `balances.inflight_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Net pending transaction amount (cents). Represents authorized but unsettled transactions (e.g., card authorizations). Managed by Blnk's QUE‚Ä¶ |
| `balances.inflight_credit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Pending inbound credits (cents). |
| `balances.inflight_debit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Pending outbound debits (cents). |
| `bookkeeping` | object | ‚úì |  |  | Shadow bookkeeping layer fields for 5300 regulatory reporting and FBO segregation. These are managed by Cassandra, not sourced from Blnk. |
| `bookkeeping.account_code_5300` | string |  |  |  | NCUA 5300 account code (e.g., 025B for share drafts, 010 for regular shares). Assigned automatically based on account type; overridable by‚Ä¶ |
| `bookkeeping.schedule_a_code` | string |  |  |  | 5300 Schedule A line item code for balance sheet classification. Used by the 5300 Reporter consumer for aggregated reporting. |
| `bookkeeping.fbo_classification` | string (enum: customer_balance, commingled_cash, settlement) |  |  |  | FBO sub-account classification for the bookkeeping layer. - `customer_balance` ‚Äî Holds attributed to a specific end-user entity. - `comming‚Ä¶ |
| `bookkeeping.locked_amount` | integer |  |  |  | Amount held by BSA/compliance controls (cents). Separate from Blnk inflight holds ‚Äî this represents funds frozen by the control engine (e.g‚Ä¶ |
| `owners` | array<object> | ‚úì |  |  | All entities with ownership or authority over this account. At minimum, includes the primary owner matching entity_id. Joint ownership is m‚Ä¶ |
| `blnk_ledger_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding ledger ID in Blnk Deploy. Opaque reference for reconciliation. |
| `account_numbers` | array<object> |  |  |  | Summary of account numbers associated with this account. Use the Account Numbers endpoints for full details and management. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  | Arbitrary key-value pairs for partner use. |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `account_number`  (schema: `AccountNumber` ¬∑ 10 fields ¬∑ retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique account number resource identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The ledger account this number is associated with. Immutable. |
| `account_number` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | 12-digit account number allocated by the aggregator. Format is PPP-SSSSSSSS-C where P is the 3-digit fintech prefix, S is the 8-digit seque‚Ä¶ |
| `routing_number` | string | ‚úì |  |  | ABA routing number. All account numbers within a credit union share the same routing number (the credit union's Fed master account routing‚Ä¶ |
| `status` | string (enum: active, disabled, canceled) | ‚úì |  |  | Account number lifecycle state. - `active` ‚Äî Routable. Inbound payments accepted, outbound allowed. - `disabled` ‚Äî Temporarily non-routable‚Ä¶ |
| `informational_entity_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Entity ID for FBO attribution. Typically matches the account's primary owner but can differ in BaaS scenarios where the fintech partner is‚Ä¶ |
| `label` | string |  |  |  | Human-readable label for this account number (e.g., Primary Checking, Partner A Routing). For partner reference only, not used in payment f‚Ä¶ |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |

### `ach_transfer`  (schema: `AchTransfer` ¬∑ 23 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique ACH transfer identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The ledger account being debited or credited. |
| `account_number_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The specific account number used for this transfer. Determines the routing/account number pair that appears in the ACH file. |
| `direction` | string (enum: credit, debit) | ‚úì |  |  | Direction of the ACH transfer from the perspective of this account. - `credit` ‚Äî Push money out (originate a payment to an external party).‚Ä¶ |
| `amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Transfer amount in cents. Must be positive. |
| `currency` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending_approval, submitted, settled, returned, rejected, canceled) | ‚úì |  |  | ACH transfer lifecycle state. - `pending_approval` ‚Äî Created, awaiting control engine evaluation. - `submitted` ‚Äî Controls passed, ACH file‚Ä¶ |
| `window` | string (enum: standard, same_day) | ‚úì |  |  | ACH processing window. - `standard` ‚Äî Next-day settlement (standard ACH). - `same_day` ‚Äî Same-day ACH (additional fee may apply, cutoff tim‚Ä¶ |
| `counterparty` | object | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `counterparty.routing_number` | string | ‚úì |  |  | ABA routing number of the receiving institution. |
| `counterparty.account_number` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | Account number at the receiving institution. |
| `counterparty.account_type` | string (enum: checking, savings) | ‚úì |  |  | Account type at the receiving institution. |
| `counterparty.name` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | Receiver name as it will appear in the ACH file. Max 22 characters per NACHA format. |
| `description` | string |  |  |  | Company entry description in the ACH batch header. Max 10 characters per NACHA format (e.g., "PAYROLL", "PAYMENT"). |
| `company_name` | string |  |  |  | Originator company name in the ACH batch header. Max 16 characters per NACHA format. Defaults to partner's registered name. |
| `effective_date` | string |  |  |  | The date the transfer will settle at the Fed. Set by the system based on the processing window and submission time. For same-day ACH, this‚Ä¶ |
| `expected_settlement` | string |  |  |  | Estimated settlement timestamp. More precise than effective_date ‚Äî accounts for Fed processing windows, weekends, and holidays. |
| `settled_at` | string |  |  |  | Actual settlement timestamp. Populated when status becomes `settled`. |
| `returned_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp of the return. Populated when status becomes `returned`. |
| `return_code` | string |  |  |  | NACHA return reason code (e.g., R01 Insufficient Funds, R02 Account Closed, R03 No Account, R04 Invalid Account Number). Only present when‚Ä¶ |
| `return_reason` | string |  |  |  | Human-readable return reason. Only present when status is `returned`. |
| `rejection_reason` | string |  |  |  | Explanation of why the control engine rejected this transfer. Only present when status is `rejected`. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Results of all control engine evaluations for this transfer. Populated at creation time (when the control engine gates the transfer). |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique book transfer identifier. |
| `source_account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The account being debited. |
| `destination_account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The account being credited. |
| `amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Transfer amount in cents. |
| `currency` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, completed, rejected) | ‚úì |  |  | Book transfer lifecycle state. - `pending` ‚Äî Created, control engine evaluating. - `completed` ‚Äî Funds moved instantly (both sides settled)‚Ä¶ |
| `description` | string |  |  |  | Free-text description of the transfer purpose. |
| `rejection_reason` | string |  |  |  | Explanation of why the control engine rejected this transfer. Only present when status is `rejected`. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Results of control engine evaluations. |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |

### `wire_transfer`  (schema: `WireTransfer` ¬∑ 21 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique wire transfer identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The ledger account being debited (outbound) or credited (inbound). |
| `account_number_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The account number used for this wire. |
| `direction` | string (enum: outbound, inbound) | ‚úì |  |  | Direction of the wire transfer. - `outbound` ‚Äî Originated by this instance, sending funds via Fedwire. - `inbound` ‚Äî Received from an exter‚Ä¶ |
| `amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Transfer amount in cents. |
| `currency` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending_approval, submitted, completed, return_requested, returned, rejected, ‚Ä¶(+1)) | ‚úì |  |  | Wire transfer lifecycle state. - `pending_approval` ‚Äî Created, awaiting control engine evaluation. - `submitted` ‚Äî Controls passed, Fedwire‚Ä¶ |
| `beneficiary` | object |  |  |  | The receiving party and their bank details for an outbound wire. |
| `beneficiary.name` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | Beneficiary name as it will appear on the Fedwire message. |
| `beneficiary.routing_number` | string | ‚úì |  |  | ABA routing number of the beneficiary's bank. |
| `beneficiary.account_number` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | Account number at the beneficiary's bank. |
| `beneficiary.bank_name` | string |  |  |  | Name of the beneficiary's bank. Optional ‚Äî if omitted, resolved automatically from the routing number via the Fed directory. |
| `beneficiary.address` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `beneficiary.address.line1` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | Street address line 1. |
| `beneficiary.address.line2` | string |  |  |  | Street address line 2 (apt, suite, unit, etc.). |
| `beneficiary.address.city` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  |  |
| `beneficiary.address.state` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Two-letter US state or territory code (ISO 3166-2:US). |
| `beneficiary.address.postal_code` | string | ‚úì | ‚úì |  | US ZIP code (5-digit or ZIP+4). |
| `beneficiary.address.country` | string | ‚úì |  |  | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. |
| `originator` | object |  |  |  | Originator information from an inbound Fedwire message. Read-only ‚Äî populated by the aggregator when routing an inbound wire. |
| `originator.name` | string |  | ‚úì |  |  |
| `originator.routing_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.account_number` | string |  | ‚úì |  |  |
| `originator.bank_name` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.reference` | string |  |  |  | Originator-to-beneficiary information (OBI) from the Fedwire message. |
| `purpose` | string |  |  |  | Originator-to-beneficiary information (OBI). Free-text description of the wire purpose, included in the Fedwire message. |
| `imad` | string |  |  |  | Input Message Accountability Data ‚Äî the Fed's unique identifier for this Fedwire message. Populated after submission. Used for tracing and‚Ä¶ |
| `omad` | string |  |  |  | Output Message Accountability Data ‚Äî assigned by the receiving Fed bank. Populated after the wire is acknowledged. |
| `submitted_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp when the Fedwire message was submitted. |
| `completed_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp when the wire completed. Wires are typically same-day. |
| `return_request` | object |  |  |  | Details of the return request, if one has been filed. Only present when status is `return_requested` or `returned`. |
| `rejection_reason` | string |  |  |  | Explanation of why the control engine rejected this wire. Only present when status is `rejected`. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Results of control engine evaluations. |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `verification`  (schema: `Verification` ¬∑ 14 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique verification identifier. |
| `entity_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The entity being verified. |
| `type` | string (enum: kyc, kyb) | ‚úì |  |  | Whether this is identity verification for a person (KYC) or a business (KYB). Trusts use KYB. Joint entities are verified through their ind‚Ä¶ |
| `provider` | string (enum: alloy, persona, socure, middesk, partner_provided) | ‚úì |  |  | The identity verification provider used for this check. - `alloy`, `persona`, `socure`, `middesk` ‚Äî Cassandra-managed providers. The system‚Ä¶ |
| `trust_level` | string (enum: full, verify_watchlist_only) |  |  |  | Trust level for partner-provided verifications (from partner configuration). - `full` ‚Äî Cassandra trusts the partner's identity verificatio‚Ä¶ |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, in_review, approved, denied, expired) | ‚úì |  |  | Status of this individual verification attempt. - `pending` ‚Äî Verification submitted to provider, awaiting result. - `in_review` ‚Äî Provider‚Ä¶ |
| `ofac_result` | object | ‚úì |  |  | Result of the mandatory OFAC screening. OFAC screening is a compliance floor control (BA-05) and runs on every verification regardless of p‚Ä¶ |
| `ofac_result.screened_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `ofac_result.match_status` | string (enum: clear, potential_match, confirmed_match) |  |  |  | - `clear` ‚Äî No matches found on SDN, consolidated sanctions, or PEP lists. - `potential_match` ‚Äî Possible match requiring compliance office‚Ä¶ |
| `ofac_result.matched_lists` | array<string> |  |  |  | Names of lists that returned matches (e.g., "SDN", "Consolidated Sanctions", "PEP", "314a"). Empty array when match_status is `clear`. |
| `ofac_result.match_score` | number |  |  |  | Confidence score of the strongest match (0-100). Only meaningful when match_status is `potential_match`. Used by compliance officers to pri‚Ä¶ |
| `provider_result` | object |  |  |  | Normalized verification result from the provider. Cassandra translates each provider's native response into this common structure. |
| `provider_result.provider_reference` | string |  |  |  | The provider's native reference ID for this verification (e.g., Alloy evaluation_token, Persona inquiry_id). |
| `provider_result.provider_status` | string |  |  |  | The provider's native status string, preserved for debugging. Cassandra normalizes this into the verification's top-level `status`. |
| `provider_result.identity_verified` | boolean |  |  |  | Whether the provider confirmed the identity matches the provided data. |
| `provider_result.document_verified` | boolean |  |  |  | Whether identity documents were verified (if required by risk tier). Null if document verification was not required. |
| `provider_result.risk_score` | number |  |  |  | Provider's risk score, normalized to 0-100 range. Higher = riskier. Used by CDD controls for risk tiering. |
| `provider_result.risk_signals` | array<string> |  |  |  | Risk signal tags from the provider (e.g., "address_mismatch", "ssn_issued_prior_to_dob", "high_risk_jurisdiction"). Used by the control eng‚Ä¶ |
| `provider_result.raw_response` | object |  |  |  | The complete provider response, stored verbatim for audit purposes. Only returned when the requesting token has compliance-scoped permissio‚Ä¶ |
| `denial_reasons` | array<string> |  |  |  | Machine-readable reasons for denial (e.g., "identity_not_verified", "document_mismatch", "high_risk_jurisdiction", "ofac_match"). Only pres‚Ä¶ |
| `expires_at` | string |  |  |  | When this verification result expires per CDD refresh policy. After expiry, the entity's kyc_status transitions to `expired` and a new veri‚Ä¶ |
| `triggered_by` | string (enum: initial, cdd_refresh, information_change, manual) |  |  |  | What triggered this verification. - `initial` ‚Äî First-time verification on entity creation. - `cdd_refresh` ‚Äî Periodic re-verification per‚Ä¶ |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `card`  (schema: `Card` ¬∑ 16 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique card identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The ledger account this card draws from / credits to. |
| `entity_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The entity this card is issued to. Must be a person entity. Immutable after creation. |
| `card_type` | string (enum: virtual, physical) | ‚úì |  |  | - `virtual` ‚Äî Instantly issued, PAN available immediately via secure details endpoint. - `physical` ‚Äî Requires fulfillment (mailing). PAN a‚Ä¶ |
| `form_factor` | string (enum: debit, prepaid) | ‚úì |  |  | Card form factor for network classification. - `debit` ‚Äî Linked to a deposit account with overdraft rules. - `prepaid` ‚Äî Balance-limited, n‚Ä¶ |
| `network` | string (enum: visa, mastercard) |  |  |  | Card network, determined by the BIN allocated to this instance. |
| `status` | string (enum: inactive, active, frozen, closed) | ‚úì |  |  | Card lifecycle state, driven by the adapter interface (createCard, activateCard, freezeCard, closeCard). - `inactive` ‚Äî Card created but no‚Ä¶ |
| `last_four` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Last four digits of the card number (PAN). |
| `expiration_month` | integer | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `expiration_year` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Four-digit expiration year. |
| `shipping` | object |  |  |  | Shipping details for physical cards. Only present when `card_type` is `physical`. Null for virtual cards. |
| `shipping.status` | string (enum: pending, shipped, delivered, returned) |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.tracking_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.carrier` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.shipped_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.estimated_delivery` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `spend_controls` | object |  |  |  | Optional per-card spending limits. These are enforced in addition to account-level controls and the control engine's velocity limits. |
| `spend_controls.daily_limit` | integer |  |  |  | Maximum daily spend in cents. Null means no per-card daily limit. |
| `spend_controls.monthly_limit` | integer |  |  |  | Maximum monthly spend in cents. |
| `spend_controls.per_transaction_limit` | integer |  |  |  | Maximum single transaction amount in cents. |
| `spend_controls.allowed_mcc_codes` | array<string> |  |  |  | Whitelist of allowed Merchant Category Codes. If populated, only transactions at merchants with these MCCs are approved. Empty array means‚Ä¶ |
| `spend_controls.blocked_mcc_codes` | array<string> |  |  |  | Blacklist of blocked MCCs. Transactions at merchants with these MCCs are declined. Evaluated after allowed_mcc_codes. |
| `processor_reference` | string |  |  |  | Card reference ID at the processor (Marqeta card_token or Lithic card_id). Opaque ‚Äî used internally for adapter operations. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `card_authorization`  (schema: `CardAuthorization` ¬∑ 14 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `card_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, approved, declined, reversed, expired) | ‚úì |  |  | Authorization lifecycle. - `pending` ‚Äî Authorization received, control engine evaluating (transient, sub-2-second). - `approved` ‚Äî Authoriz‚Ä¶ |
| `amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Authorization amount in cents. |
| `currency` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `merchant` | object | ‚úì |  |  | Merchant details from the authorization request. |
| `merchant.name` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.mcc` | string |  |  |  | Merchant Category Code. |
| `merchant.city` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.state` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.country` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.acceptor_id` | string |  |  |  | Card acceptor ID from the processor. |
| `decline_reason` | string (enum: insufficient_funds, card_frozen, card_closed, account_frozen, account_locked, spend_limit_exceeded, ‚Ä¶(+5)) |  |  |  | Machine-readable reason for declining an authorization. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Control engine results for this authorization. Includes balance check, velocity, OFAC, fraud, and spend control evaluations. |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Blnk inflight transaction ID (for approved authorizations). Null for declined authorizations. |
| `processor_reference` | string |  |  |  | Processor's native authorization reference. |
| `settled_at` | string |  |  |  | Settlement timestamp. Populated when the inflight transaction commits in Blnk (processor sends settlement webhook). Null until settled. |
| `settlement_amount` | integer |  |  |  | Final settlement amount in cents. May differ from the authorization amount (e.g., tip added, partial fulfillment). |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `bookkeeping_entry`  (schema: `BookkeepingEntry` ¬∑ 17 fields ¬∑ retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique bookkeeping entry identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The ledger account this entry pertains to. |
| `account_number_id` | string |  |  |  | The specific account number involved (if applicable). Null for account-level entries (e.g., interest, fees). |
| `entity_id` | string |  |  |  | The entity attributed for FBO segregation. Derived from the account's primary owner or the account number's informational_entity_id, depend‚Ä¶ |
| `entry_type` | string (enum: debit, credit, hold_placed, hold_released, hold_seized) | ‚úì |  |  | Type of bookkeeping entry. - `debit` ‚Äî Funds left the account (matches a Blnk debit transaction). - `credit` ‚Äî Funds entered the account (m‚Ä¶ |
| `source` | string (enum: ach_transfer, wire_transfer, book_transfer, card_authorization, card_settlement, fee, ‚Ä¶(+3)) | ‚úì |  |  | The originating transaction type that produced this bookkeeping entry. Maps 1:1 to the domain event that triggered the Blnk webhook. |
| `source_id` | string |  |  |  | ID of the originating resource (e.g., ach_5kRb3m for an ACH transfer, auth_2nRb7m for a card authorization). Allows joining bookkeeping ent‚Ä¶ |
| `amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Entry amount in cents. Always positive ‚Äî direction is conveyed by entry_type. |
| `currency` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `running_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Account balance after this entry was applied (cents). Provides a verifiable audit trail ‚Äî each entry's running_balance should equal the pre‚Ä¶ |
| `account_code_5300` | string | ‚úì |  |  | NCUA 5300 account code for this entry (e.g., 025B for share drafts, 010 for regular shares). Inherited from the account's bookkeeping tags‚Ä¶ |
| `schedule_a_code` | string |  |  |  | 5300 Schedule A line item code for balance sheet classification. Used by the 5300 Reporter for aggregated reporting. |
| `fbo_classification` | string (enum: customer_balance, commingled_cash, settlement) | ‚úì |  |  | FBO sub-account classification for the bookkeeping layer. - `customer_balance` ‚Äî Holds attributed to a specific end-user entity. - `comming‚Ä¶ |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding Blnk Deploy transaction ID. Null for compliance hold entries (which are Cassandra-native, not Blnk transactions). |
| `description` | string |  |  |  | System-generated description of the entry (e.g., "ACH credit from John Doe via 271070801", "Compliance hold placed ‚Äî BA-05 OFAC match"). |
| `effective_date` | string |  |  |  | The business date this entry is effective for regulatory reporting. May differ from created_at for entries posted after business hours or f‚Ä¶ |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `event`  (schema: `Event` ¬∑ 11 fields ¬∑ retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique event identifier. |
| `type` | string (enum: entity.created, entity.activated, entity.disabled, entity.archived, entity.updated, entity.locked, ‚Ä¶(+51)) | ‚úì |  |  | Domain event type. Events are emitted on every state transition and significant operation. Partners receive these via webhook delivery. |
| `resource_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | ID of the resource that changed (e.g., ent_abc123, acct_xyz789). |
| `resource_type` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Type of the resource (e.g., entity, account, ach_transfer). |
| `data` | object |  |  |  | Snapshot of the resource at the time of the event. Contains the full resource representation (same schema as the corresponding GET endpoint‚Ä¶ |
| `previous_data` | object |  |  |  | For update events, the resource state before the change. Only includes fields that changed. Null for creation events. |
| `delivery_status` | string (enum: pending, delivered, failed, expired) |  |  |  | Delivery status for a specific webhook delivery attempt. - `pending` ‚Äî Queued for delivery (or retrying). - `delivered` ‚Äî Partner endpoint‚Ä¶ |
| `delivery_attempts` | integer |  |  |  | Number of webhook delivery attempts made so far (max 12). |
| `last_delivery_attempt_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp of the most recent delivery attempt. |
| `delivered_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp of successful delivery. Null if not yet delivered. |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `webhook_endpoint`  (schema: `WebhookEndpoint` ¬∑ 9 fields)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Unique webhook endpoint identifier. |
| `url` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The HTTPS URL that receives webhook POST requests. Must use HTTPS (HTTP rejected). Must return 2xx within 30 seconds to be considered a suc‚Ä¶ |
| `status` | string (enum: active, disabled) | ‚úì |  |  | - `active` ‚Äî Events are delivered to this endpoint. - `disabled` ‚Äî Delivery is paused. Events accumulate and can be retrieved via the backf‚Ä¶ |
| `subscribed_events` | array<string> | ‚úì |  |  | List of event type patterns this endpoint receives. Supports exact types (e.g., "ach_transfer.settled") and wildcard prefixes (e.g., "ach_t‚Ä¶ |
| `signing_secret` | string |  |  |  | HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for verifying webhook payloads. Only returned once at creation time. Use to verify the `X-Cassandra-Signature` h‚Ä¶ |
| `delivery_stats` | object |  |  |  | Rolling delivery statistics for this endpoint. |
| `delivery_stats.total_delivered` | integer |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.total_failed` | integer |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.last_delivered_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.last_failed_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.current_failure_streak` | integer |  |  |  | Consecutive failed deliveries. When this reaches a threshold, the endpoint may be automatically disabled and the partner alerted. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `fbo_position`  (schema: `FboPosition` ¬∑ 6 fields)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `instance_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `balance` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Current FBO balance for this instance (cents). Sum of all settled inbound minus all settled outbound for this instance's accounts. |
| `held_amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Amount currently reserved for pending outbound payments (cents). Held via the reserve saga pattern (Decision 28, step 4). |
| `available_balance` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | Effective available balance (cents). Computed as `balance - held_amount`. This is the amount the Origination API checks against when approv‚Ä¶ |
| `last_event_sequence` | integer | ‚úì |  |  | The sequence_id of the most recent event processed by the Payment Hub consumer for this instance. Used for staleness detection. |
| `updated_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

### `inbound_payment`  (schema: `InboundPayment` ¬∑ 9 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `payment_type` | string (enum: ach_credit, ach_debit, wire, rtp) | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `account_number` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The 12-digit account number this payment was routed to. |
| `amount` | integer | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `currency` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, delivered, acknowledged) | ‚úì |  |  | - `pending` ‚Äî Aggregator has the payment, not yet pushed to instance. - `delivered` ‚Äî Pushed to instance via webhook. - `acknowledged` ‚Äî In‚Ä¶ |
| `originator` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.name` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.routing_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.account_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `reference` | string |  |  |  | Originator reference or trace number. |
| `received_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  | When the aggregator received this payment from the Fed. |

### `bsa_alert`  (schema: `BsaAlert` ¬∑ 9 fields ¬∑ retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `alert_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |
| `event_id` | string | ‚úì |  |  | The event that triggered this alert. |
| `alert_type` | string (enum: ctr_threshold, structuring_suspected, cross_fintech_activity, sar_candidate, velocity_anomaly, watchlist_hit) | ‚úì |  |  | Type of BSA alert generated by the BSA Approver (real-time) or BSA Reporter (scheduled). |
| `entity_hash` | string | ‚úì |  |  | Hashed entity identifier (SSN/EIN hash). Used for per-entity lookups without exposing PII in the alert table. |
| `instance_id` | string |  |  |  | The fintech instance where the triggering event originated. For cross-fintech alerts, this is the instance of the most recent triggering ev‚Ä¶ |
| `status` | string (enum: open, under_review, escalated, cleared, block) | ‚úì |  |  | BSA alert lifecycle. - `open` ‚Äî New alert, not yet reviewed. - `under_review` ‚Äî Analyst is investigating. - `escalated` ‚Äî Elevated to SAR c‚Ä¶ |
| `requires_lookback` | boolean |  |  |  | Whether the BSA Reporter should run a deep historical analysis on this alert. Set by the BSA Approver when it detects patterns that need 90‚Ä¶ |
| `details` | object |  |  |  | Alert-specific details. Structure varies by alert_type. Examples: transaction amounts, pattern description, matched entity information for‚Ä¶ |
| `created_at` | string | ‚úì |  |  |  |

## Events

> ‚öÝÔ∏è  **Vocabulary warning ‚Äî no events defined.** The `events` array in vocabulary.json is empty. Any `(event.code)` reference the policy wants to use must be introduced by engineering and registered in the spec before the Design Overlay v2 blocks can cite it. The endpoints list below is provided as candidate signals from which engineering could derive event codes (e.g., `POST /accounts` ‚Üí `account.created`).

## Endpoints

| Method | Path | Summary | Control refs | Audit events |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/entities` | List entities |  |  |
| `GET` | `/entities/{entity_id}` | Retrieve an entity |  |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/person` | Create a person entity | CD-01, CD-04, BA-03, BA-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/business` | Create a business entity | CD-01, CD-04, CD-05, BA-03, BA-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/trust` | Create a trust entity | CD-01, CD-04, BA-03, BA-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/joint` | Create a joint entity | BA-05 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/person/{entity_id}` | Update a person entity | CD-09, BA-05 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/business/{entity_id}` | Update a business entity | CD-05, CD-09 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/trust/{entity_id}` | Update a trust entity | CD-09 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/joint/{entity_id}` | Update a joint entity |  |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/{entity_id}/status` | Transition entity status | BA-05, CD-08 |  |
| `PUT` | `/entities/{entity_id}/lock` | Update entity lock |  |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts` | List accounts |  |  |
| `POST` | `/accounts` | Create an account | CD-04, CD-03, CD-12, BA-05 |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{account_id}` | Retrieve an account |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/accounts/{account_id}` | Update an account | CD-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/accounts/{account_id}/status` | Transition account status | CD-12 |  |
| `PUT` | `/accounts/{account_id}/lock` | Update account lock |  |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{account_id}/account-numbers` | List account numbers for an account |  |  |
| `POST` | `/accounts/{account_id}/account-numbers` | Create an additional account number | BA-05 |  |
| `GET` | `/account-numbers/{account_number_id}` | Retrieve an account number |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/account-numbers/{account_number_id}` | Update an account number |  |  |
| `POST` | `/account-numbers/{account_number_id}/status` | Transition account number status |  |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/ach` | List ACH transfers |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/ach` | Create an ACH transfer | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10, CD-06 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/ach/{ach_transfer_id}` | Retrieve an ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/ach/{ach_transfer_id}/cancel` | Cancel an ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/ach/batch` | Create a batch of ACH transfers | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10, CD-06 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/book` | List book transfers |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/book` | Create a book transfer | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/book/{book_transfer_id}` | Retrieve a book transfer |  |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/wire` | List wire transfers |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/wire` | Create a wire transfer | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/wire/{wire_transfer_id}` | Retrieve a wire transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/wire/{wire_transfer_id}/cancel` | Cancel a wire transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/wire/{wire_transfer_id}/return-request` | Request a wire return |  |  |
| `GET` | `/entities/{entity_id}/verifications` | List verifications for an entity |  |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/{entity_id}/verifications` | Initiate a verification | CD-01, CD-04, BA-03, BA-05, BA-20 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/{entity_id}/verifications/attest` | Submit a partner-provided KYC/KYB attestation | CD-01, CD-10, BA-05, BA-20 |  |
| `GET` | `/verifications/{verification_id}` | Retrieve a verification |  |  |
| `GET` | `/cards` | List cards |  |  |
| `POST` | `/cards` | Create a card | BA-05, CD-04 |  |
| `GET` | `/cards/{card_id}` | Retrieve a card |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/cards/{card_id}` | Update a card |  |  |
| `POST` | `/cards/{card_id}/status` | Transition card status |  |  |
| `GET` | `/cards/{card_id}/sensitive` | Retrieve sensitive card details | PR-01 |  |
| `GET` | `/cards/{card_id}/authorizations` | List authorizations for a card |  |  |
| `GET` | `/authorizations/{authorization_id}` | Retrieve a card authorization |  |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{account_id}/bookkeeping-entries` | List bookkeeping entries for an account |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bookkeeping-entries/{entry_id}` | Retrieve a bookkeeping entry |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bookkeeping/summary` | Get aggregated bookkeeping summary |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bookkeeping/reconciliation` | Run reconciliation check |  |  |
| `GET` | `/events` | List events |  |  |
| `GET` | `/events/{event_id}` | Retrieve an event |  |  |
| `POST` | `/events/{event_id}/retry` | Retry event delivery |  |  |
| `GET` | `/webhooks` | List webhook endpoints |  |  |
| `POST` | `/webhooks` | Create a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `GET` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}` | Retrieve a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}` | Update a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `DELETE` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}` | Delete a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `POST` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}/rotate-secret` | Rotate webhook signing secret |  |  |
| `POST` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}/test` | Send a test webhook |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/kyc/approve` | Simulate: Approve KYC verification |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/kyc/deny` | Simulate: Deny KYC verification |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/ach/receive` | Simulate: Receive an inbound ACH credit |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/ach/settle` | Simulate: Settle an outbound ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/ach/return` | Simulate: Return a settled ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/wire/receive` | Simulate: Receive an inbound wire |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/wire/settle` | Simulate: Complete an outbound wire |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/card/authorize` | Simulate: Card authorization |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/card/settle` | Simulate: Card settlement |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/time/advance` | Simulate: Advance sandbox clock |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/reset` | Reset sandbox environment |  |  |
| `POST` | `/auth/token` | Obtain an aggregator access token |  |  |
| `GET` | `/fbo/{instance_id}/position` | Get FBO position for an instance |  |  |
| `GET` | `/fbo/{instance_id}/available-balance` | Get available balance for an instance |  |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/ach/originate` | Originate an ACH payment | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08 |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/wire/originate` | Originate a wire payment | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10 |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/wire/prepare` | Prepare a wire payment (phase 1 of 2) | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10 |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/wire/confirm` | Confirm a prepared wire payment (phase 2 of 2) |  |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/rtp/originate` | Originate an RTP payment | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08 |  |
| `GET` | `/inbound/{instance_id}` | List inbound payments for an instance |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bsa/alerts` | List BSA alerts |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bsa/alerts/{alert_id}` | Retrieve a BSA alert |  |  |
| `GET` | `/consumers/health` | Get aggregator consumer health |  |  |

## State machines

_No state machines defined in vocabulary.json._

## Plugins

_No plugins defined in vocabulary.json._
"""


LOCAL OVERRIDES (apply after all meta-prompt rules):
{{Usually empty. Put anything here that should override the shared
`meta-prompt.md` for THIS policy only ‚Äî a different control-ID prefix,
extra required sections, a specific citation style, etc. The regeneration
task applies overrides AFTER the shared meta-prompt, so instructions here
win.}}
